title: call-agent parent_grant and requested_grant are Type.Optional but functionally required when agent declares tools — silent default-empty triggers grant violation
description: callAgentTool.parameters at packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/call-agent-tool.ts lines 48-56 declares both parent_grant and requested_grant as Type.Optional(Type.Array(Type.String())). computeGrantViolation at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/jit-runtime.ts lines 90-95 treats absent grants as empty sets: `parentGrant ?? []`. When an agent's spec declares `tools: [read]` and call-agent is invoked without passing parent_grant, the intersection is empty and GrantViolationError fires with message 'Agent X grant violation: tools [read] not in parent grant (DEC-0047 clamp at executeAgent boundary)'. The type-optional declaration does not match the dispatch-required semantics; no orchestration-context fallback (e.g. caller's own tool set via ExtensionContext) is consulted.

evidence.reference: FB-008 'call-agent requires explicit parent_grant and requested_grant'; actual_behavior: agent with tools: [read] dispatched without parent_grant → 'Agent X grant violation: tools [read] not in parent grant (DEC-0047 clamp at executeAgent boundary)'

Investigation report FB-008 section confirms both surfaces verbatim (call-agent-tool.ts:48-56 Type.Optional declaration; jit-runtime.ts:90-95 default-empty handling); verdict 'Tool surface or DEC' — describe parent_grant as required-when-tools-declared in the schema, or compute parent_grant from orchestration context (ExtensionContext) when absent
parent_grant: Type.Optional(...) requested_grant: Type.Optional(...) — both declared optional in the tool parameter schema
computeGrantViolation: const parent = parentGrant ?? []; — silent default-empty when caller does not pass parent_grant

impact: First-time call-agent invocations for tool-enabled agent specs hard-fail at the grant clamp. The error message names the DEC-0047 clamp but not the workflow contract (always pass parent_grant). Authors cannot discover the required-when-tools semantic from the tool schema. The semantic is correct per the capability-clamp design (default-empty per DEC-0047) but the surface is mis-aligned: type-optional read as 'safe to omit' contradicts dispatch-required read as 'omission means zero grant'.
proposed_resolution: Tool surface at packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/call-agent-tool.ts lines 48-56: parent_grant description states 'Required when the agent spec declares tools. Defaults to empty array; sub-agent tools must be an explicit subset of this grant per DEC-0047 capability-clamp.' OR: extend call-agent execute to compute parent_grant from ExtensionContext when absent, deriving the caller's full tool set as the default parent grant. DEC-level decision required between schema-documentation and runtime-derivation paths.
